concise_thesis.negative_result: No mature second near-breach chain was observed in the measured artifacts.
non_claims: This is not a proof of Collatz.
This does not establish a global invariant.
This does not exclude all possible divergent behavior.
This is bounded measurement evidence only.
concise_thesis.one_sentence: In bounded odd-step Collatz measurements, debt accumulation tends to trigger fast release, post-response debt generally falls below prior peaks, near-breach grammars exist but appear isolated, and apparent fuzzy breaches were reclassified as weak-prefix artifacts rather than mature regeneration chains.

findings.boundary: Correlation is not proof and does not imply global closure.
findings.interpretation: Higher bounded debt peaks tend to be followed by stronger or more massive release behavior.
findings.name: Debt creates measurable release pressure

findings.boundary: A positive bounded floor is not a theorem and cannot be extrapolated as an invariant.
findings.interpretation: The debt-release correlation remains positive in the tested ranges, but the seed-scale axis weakens it.
findings.name: The pressure signal is scale-positive but decays with seed scale

findings.boundary: A weak positive band is descriptive only.
findings.interpretation: The signal does not vanish in the tested local bands, but it becomes weak and should not be overstated.
findings.name: After the initial drop, local seed blocks remain positive but weak

findings.boundary: Stable response timing is not a termination proof.
findings.interpretation: Across measured blocks, the first post-peak response usually appears immediately or very quickly.
findings.name: Response time is highly stable

findings.boundary: Post-response reduction is bounded evidence only.
findings.interpretation: Once response occurs, bounded debt typically reduces rather than forming a new peak.
findings.name: Post-response debt usually collapses below the prior peak

findings.boundary: No bounded new peak is not global impossibility.
findings.interpretation: Increasing bounded horizon length did not create post-response new-peak behavior in the tested range.
findings.name: Extended horizons did not produce new post-response peaks

findings.boundary: Near-breach is not divergence and not a proof boundary.
findings.interpretation: Some trajectories nearly rebuild prior debt, but no true breach was observed in the bounded set.
findings.name: Near-breach candidates exist but remain below breach

findings.boundary: Grammar isolation is a bounded observation, not a global theorem.
findings.interpretation: Near-breach windows are driven by expansion-rich grammar, but measured recurrences did not chain.
findings.name: Near-breach grammar is expansion-rich but isolated

findings.boundary: Fuzzy absence of chains is not a global exclusion.
findings.interpretation: Relaxing exact grammar increases detected pattern hits, but did not generate recurring dangerous chains in the bounded test.
findings.name: Fuzzy grammar increases hits but not dangerous chains

findings.boundary: Correcting false positives strengthens measurement language but does not prove Collatz.
findings.interpretation: The raw v6.3 breach signal was caused by low prior peaks, not by mature debt regeneration.
findings.name: Apparent fuzzy breaches were weak-prefix artifacts

public_positioning.best_short_label: bounded structural measurement of Collatz odd-step debt/release grammar
public_positioning.strongest_current_claim: Within the measured bounded artifacts, no persistent mature low-compression regeneration chain was observed.
public_positioning.weakest_current_point: The work remains empirical and bounded; larger scale, independent replication, and formalization are needed.
what_is_new_inside_this_repo: A native debt/release vocabulary was made computable.
Post-peak release pressure was measured across scale and horizon variations.
Response timing and post-response debt survival were separated from raw correlation.
Near-breach grammar was isolated and then guarded against fuzzy false positives.
Weak-prefix rebound was separated from mature-debt rebound.
